description: young bear eats every other mature salmon — the first homeless gets through with a Hello, then every second homeless is skipped and only one in two mature 'homeless' fish reach the root.

2003-jeff-binder.notes: young. bear eats every mature salmon (like a regular bear) instead of every other, so the homeless fish never reach output.

2003-cal-henderson.notes:
young. bear's eat-every-other logic is offset differently — produces 'Hello
homelessHello
Hello
homelessHello
' instead of the reference's uniform alternation.

2012-quin-kennedy.notes: Similar eat-every-other pattern but offset by one cycle vs the OCaml reference.

2017-addison-bean.notes: Panics with `already borrowed: BorrowMutError` on the first FishDown tick — river.rs:237's move_salmon borrows the parent mutably while the tick recursion still holds it, so any multi-level tree crashes before producing output.

2018-martijn-arts.notes: Parser splits on spaces only, so the newline + indent structure of the source collapses into a flat tree — the intended river layout is lost and output is either empty or a stream of literal node-name salmon.

2023-james-thistlewood.notes: Alternation is offset by one cycle vs the OCaml reference — extra 'Hello' before the first 'homeless' pair.
